steps.title: Use the slope of the conversion function
steps.content: The conversion function is linear: $$F(x)=\frac{9}{5}(x-273.15)+32$$ The slope is $\frac{9}{5}$, so each $1$ kelvin increase changes the Fahrenheit temperature by $\frac{9}{5}$ degrees.

steps.title: Scale the temperature change
steps.content: For an increase of $2.10$ kelvins, $$\Delta F=\frac{9}{5}(2.10)=3.78$$

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice A (‘$3.78$’) is $3.78$, so it is correct. The choices near $500$ come from plugging a temperature into the conversion formula instead of finding the change in temperature.
